title: Provider Environment Classification Semantic Contract
description: Coverage-complete semantic contract for the provider environment classification capability. Semantic information captured as a USF coverage record via the coverage record and equivalence audit; no external source code is imported and the V2 implementation language is an open decision.
aiGuidance: Coverage-complete draft grounded in semantic information (USF coverage record only; no source import; language open). Proof is hermetic/stale lineage, not current readiness. Enrich detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Classification capability that places each provider on an environment-posture axis (hermetic, local, sandbox, live) while keeping provider mode and environment as separate honest dimensions; it is provider-backed but owns no database schema. It deploys the V1-proven env-classification matrix contract and must keep its lifecycle/readiness model unchanged during extraction, with migrations limited to contract/config references that update semantic artefacts when behaviour changes. Recovery re-runs proof:provider-environment-classification before promotion continues.

stateModel.description: The state is the classification matrix: per provider/capability, its environment posture and the separation between provider mode and environment, held as configuration rather than owned tenant rows. It has no owned database schema, so backup scope is configuration/evidence only and restore cannot create tenant data. The matrix encodes that hermetic-mock must not be read as live-external and that environment must not upgrade provider mode by implication.

permissions.description: It owns no tenant records and exposes no tenant-mutating routes, so standard platform security controls apply; its tenant impact is indirect through correct posture labelling. Classification is an operator/governance concern, not an end-user permission. The separation it enforces is what prevents a hermetic or sandbox provider from being treated as production-live.

contracts.description: The contract is the env-classification matrix that consumers read to know a provider's honest environment posture, mediated through configuration rather than a tenant API. Configuration is read from the declared provider/config plane and missing or invalid configuration fails closed instead of inventing defaults. The matrix is the authority other capabilities use to avoid overclaiming proof posture.

validation.description: Validation enforces the cross-dimension safety rules: provider mode and environment stay independent, hermetic-mock must not satisfy live-external-provider, and production-shaped must not satisfy production-live. Inputs are the per-provider posture declarations, which must be consistent and complete. Any classification that would upgrade posture by implication is rejected.

errorModel.description: Provider failure returns a typed degraded/readiness failure, keeps mutation boundaries closed, and does not fabricate successful provider state; degraded mode disables dependent side-effect paths while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible. The risk class is semantic drift or configuration loss, not tenant data loss. A blocked classification readiness or failing promotion proof is alertable.

auditModel.description: Because it owns no tenant mutation, its audit concern is the recorded classification lineage: changes to a provider's environment posture are captured as reviewable configuration/semantic changes. Readiness and audit stay visible in degraded mode. This preserves an honest, inspectable record that prevents posture inflation.

readinessModel.description: Readiness means every relevant provider has an explicit, consistent environment classification with provider mode and environment kept separate; unavailable dependencies fail closed through the readiness model. Readiness is shown by re-running proof:provider-environment-classification rather than a live heartbeat. Stale or unknown classifications do not count as ready.

proof.description: Proof is proof:provider-environment-classification, demonstrating that the matrix keeps provider mode and environment as separate, honestly classified dimensions. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No distinct UI capability at the contract level (coverage matrix ui=0); UI is modelled separately and remains a future AI-customised deliverable.
